title: To cancel certain proposed changes to loan level price adjustments by the Federal National Mortgage Association and credit fees charged by the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation.
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Financial Services.
plain_summary: This bill stops the government from putting new rules into place that would change how much people pay for home loans. In January 2023, government agencies announced they wanted to change the fees that people pay when they get a mortgage to buy a house. These new rules would make some people pay more money upfront based on their credit score and how risky their loan is. This bill says those new fee changes cannot happen and must be canceled.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Financial Services.

sections.title: Section 1 Cancellation of Changes
sections.text: The Federal Housing Finance Agency and the enterprises cannot put into effect certain changes to how they price home loans. The enterprises are the Federal National Mortgage Association and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation, which are government-sponsored companies that help make home loans available. These changes were announced on January nineteenth, two thousand and twenty-three, in a statement called Updates to the Enterprises Single-Family Pricing Framework. The changes were also described in two official letters sent to lenders in two thousand and twenty-three. This law says those changes cannot be used and have no legal power.
